issues.title: IANA considerations
issues.author: ionut-arm
issues.body: The IANA considerations section should be filled out to describe the requirements for defining, describing, and registering the media type formats that would be used within the wrapper.

issues.title: Abstract & introduction
issues.author: ionut-arm
issues.body: The Abstract/Introduction sections are fairly thin - we should add more details about the expected use cases.

issues.title: what about pre-existing CBOR tags?
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body: We need to define how to deal with CBOR tags that exist independently of a CoAP C-F.

issues.title: clearly describe encap/decap algorithm
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body: In order to avoid misunderstandings, it'd be beneficial to describe the encap/decap algorithms in a more explicit manner

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body:
Maybe add this bit from the TCG spec:

```
A parser decodes the octet string into a byte buffer
and then does a 1-byte lookahead, as illustrated in the
following pseudo code, to decide which format to use to
decode the remainder of the byte buffer:
```
```golang
switch b[0] {
case 0x82:
  return CBORArray
case 0x5b:
  return JSONArray
default:
  return CBORTag
}
```

issues.title: fix IANA section
issues.author: nedmsmith
issues.body:
Should include “This document does not require any IANA actions.”
discuss if normative for authors should exist here.

issues.title: Reuse terminology from RFC 9193
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body: RFC9193 provides an [excellent glossary](https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9193.html#name-terminology) for all things "media types".  We should stick to that.

issues.title: RFC6383 rule in the CDDL is wrong
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body:
Email from @cabo:

> The CDDL rule RFC6383 doesn’t quite mirror what RFC 6383 actually says.
> You put in a new rule for suffixes, but the way you are using “+”, these will become part of the restricted-name; maybe you need to take out
>
```
   restricted-name-chars =/ "+" ; Characters after last plus always
                                ; specify a structured syntax suffix
```

issues.title: Carl's review points
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body:
* In the third sentence of intro, message should be plural
* The definition of RFC6838 in the CDDL looks to be incomplete
* Re: suffixes, I was assuming certificate extension support would use the DER/BER values. What about CRL extensions? Is PEM needed as well?
* The structured suffix link in Section 3.1 is broken.
* Maybe reference EAT media types spec as initial values of interest.

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body: /cc @carl-wallace

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body:
* [x] In the third sentence of intro, message should be plural
* [x] The definition of RFC6838 in the CDDL looks to be incomplete
* [x] The structured suffix link in Section 3.1 is broken.
* [x] Maybe reference EAT media types spec as initial values of interest.

> Re: suffixes, I was assuming certificate extension support would use the DER/BER values. What about CRL extensions? Is PEM needed as well?

I am not sure I understand.  I'll seek clarification from Carl on the list.

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body: The leftover point is tracked in #18

comments.author: carl-wallace
comments.body: I was asking how the enumerated list of suffixes (in the -01 draft) applied to certificates and CRLs. I was assuming that ber/der signified certificates and was asking if something was needed for CRLs and whether or not PEM support was necessary. Upon re-reading, the ber/der assumption was wrong (the suffix is more general than certificates). Whether or not PEM is needed may be worth considering but it's not really relevant to this draft. I'd just ignore that comment.

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body:
Below are some suggestions/questions regarding the RATS Conceptual Messages Wrapper draft (draft-ftbs-rats-msg-wrap-00).

## ﻿Abstract
* [x] Should the spec have a broader title/focus since there appears to be nothing to limit usage to RATS conceptual messages?

> While the mechanism is generic -- at least in part, now that we added the CM indicator to the array serialisation -- we want to keep the scope limited to RATS messages.

* [x] How does this draft relate to the EAT Media Types draft? That draft states that is "defines media types to be used for Entity Attestation Token (EAT) payloads independently of the RATS Conceptual Message in which they manifest themselves." Does this mean a separate media-type would be used with the encapsulation mechanisms defined in this draft?

> This encap is intended for tunnelling RATS messages in protocols that don't use media types as first class type identifiers themselves.  The relation of this spec with the EAT media type I-D is one of "consumer": if EAT MT has a type that is good for the message, we can use it.

## Section 1
* [ ] In the first sentence of the second paragraph, what is being registered or used interoperably? This would clarify what "their" means in the second sentence. I think the media type notion might need to be introduced before this paragraph.

## Section 3.1
* [x] Is there any reason to not use `.det` with indentation of the RFC6838 definition? I think that'd be easier to read.

> To improve readability this has now been separated into its own section.

## Section 3.2
* [ ] What if a CoAP Content Format does not exist for the media type? I am assuming a media-type value is used, but it may be worth stating that.
* [ ] Are tag definitions intended to adhere to the same limitations defined for the value field in the array, i.e., `cbor-bytes / base64-string` serialized per the tag number? If not, what would govern the type?

## Section 4
* [x] I think it would be helpful to include ASCII hex of the CBOR encodings in addition to the diagnostic (i.e., `8219753144ABCDABCD` and `DA6374763244ABCDABCD`).

> Agreed; we have added the CBOR "pretty" output to the examples.

* [ ] It may be worth including a CBOR example with a media-type value. This could possibly be as a second hypothetical for which no CoAP content format has been defined or as an example with parameters of the same type as current example.
* [ ] It may be worth showing the CDDL definition of the resulting tag as it may appear in some protocol definition, i.e.. `example_tag = #6.1668576818(bstr)`.

issues.title: One remaining point from Carl
issues.author: thomas-fossati
issues.body:
This is a left over from #14 

> Re: suffixes, I was assuming certificate extension support would use the DER/BER values. What about CRL extensions? Is PEM needed as well?

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body: TODO: seek clarification from Carl on the list.

comments.author: thomas-fossati
comments.body:
@carl-wallace, I wanted to reply to your original message but I am unable to find it...

The trivial answer to your:

> What about CRL extensions? Is PEM needed as well?

would be "yes."  However, it's not clear to me whether you expect / suggest an action for CMW?

comments.body: /cc @ionut-arm
Wording should say "there are two formats" rather than "designed to have two formats"
I don't understand the editorial suggestion.  It doesn't seem to apply to L83.

Sounds like the suggested change would be along the lines of
```suggestion
There are two encapsulation formats available:
```
yes, but that doesn't work with what follows: the bulleted list describes the design criteria of the encaps, not the encaps themselves.
Ah, right, I see now!
closing this and merging.

```suggestion
The first format uses a CBOR or JSON array with two members: one for the
```

```suggestion
The first format uses a CBOR or JSON array with two members: one for the

byte string and prepends a CBOR tag to convey the type information.
```
